title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into the artery and advance a balloon-tipped catheter over this wire and into position at the side of the blockage.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Rapid inflation and deflation of the small balloon pushes the vessel wall out, reestablishing blood flow through the artery. You may feel some chest pain while this is happening.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: The same procedure is repeated for each blockage being treated.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Afterwards, your doctor will withdraw the deflated balloon and catheter from your arteries.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: Some patients will receive a coronary stent, which is a small tube made of wire mesh, designed to prop open the artery once it has been treated. A collapsed stent is placed over the deflated balloon as it's moved into position.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: When the balloon is inflated, the stent expands and locks into place.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
text_en: After the balloon and catheter are withdrawn, the stent remains behind to hold the artery open permanently.
